prompt: Create a stark, high-contrast magazine cover illustration evoking deep grief and alarm over vanishing human heritage: In a vast, cracked earthen landscape under a stormy gray sky pierced by a single beam of dying sunlight, depict exactly 25 small, weathered clay tablets inscribed with diverse ancient scripts (representing vulnerable languages) clustered together in the foreground right, intact but shadowed. To the left and dramatically rising into the sky like smoke from an inferno, show over 250 larger shards of shattered tablets exploding upward and scattering chaotically (10x more, sized twice as large for ratio emphasis), symbolizing critically endangered languages breaking apart and lost forever. On the single largest foreground tablet, inscribe a prominent, glowing script like "Yuchi" (rare US Native tongue) for context; shards bear fragmented scripts like Latin-like or rune-like forms hinting at global tongues. Use extreme contrast—dark earth vs. fiery orange shards ascending in a steep upward surge suggesting accelerating loss—with wild windswept dust heightening isolation and tragedy. Photorealistic archaeological ruin style, no people, vertical 9:16, ultra-detailed 4K, no charts/axes/legends.
dataset_name: UNESCO Atlas of the World's Languages in Danger
insight: Only about 10% of documented endangered languages are merely vulnerable (e.g., 25%), while 90% are severely/critically endangered and shattering toward extinction.
